description: Client Modernization & Push Notifications — Migrate from CRA to Vite, Bootstrap/SCSS to Tailwind + shadcn/ui, Recoil to Zustand, full UI redesign with light/dark/system theme, and add Web Push notifications

userStories.title: Migrate from CRA to Vite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the client app to build and serve with Vite so that development is faster and the webpack/CRA tooling is removed.
acceptanceCriteria: react-app-rewired, react-scripts, and config-overrides.js are removed
vite and @vitejs/plugin-react are installed
vite.config.ts is created with dev server on port 3000 and proxy to backend (VITE_BE_DOMAIN)
public/index.html is moved to index.html at the client app root (Vite convention)
tsconfig.json is updated: moduleResolution bundler, target ES2022, remove CRA-specific settings
All REACT_APP_* environment variables are renamed to VITE_* and all references updated throughout the codebase
vite-plugin-node-polyfills is installed and configured (node-forge and bip39 require Buffer/stream polyfills)
Package.json scripts updated: dev -> vite, build -> vite build, preview -> vite preview
pnpm dev --filter=@diskreta/client starts the app without errors
Existing app renders and is functional (login, register, chat all work)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui configured so that new UI components can be built with them.
acceptanceCriteria: tailwindcss and @tailwindcss/vite are installed
shadcn/ui is initialized (components.json created, src/lib/utils.ts with cn() helper exists)
src/styles/globals.css contains Tailwind directives (@import tailwindcss) and shadcn CSS variables for light and dark themes
The globals.css is imported in the app entry point
Dark mode works via class strategy (adding dark class to <html>)
A test shadcn component (e.g. Button) can be added and renders correctly with Tailwind styles
Old styling dependencies are NOT removed yet (existing UI still works alongside new setup)
shadcn path aliases (@/components, @/lib) configured in vite.config.ts and tsconfig.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up Zustand stores
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Zustand stores created to replace Recoil atoms so that state management uses a maintained library.
acceptanceCriteria: zustand is installed
src/stores/auth.ts is created with the same shape as the current userState atom (LoggedUser | null) with getters and setters
src/stores/chats.ts is created with the same shape as chatsState atom (Record<string, Chat> | null) with methods for updating messages, status, reactions, typing
src/stores/ui.ts is created combining: theme (light/dark/system), dialog state, focus state, timestamp display state, replyingTo state, sessionTimeout

Encrypted localStorage persistence middleware is implemented in src/stores/middleware/encrypted-storage.ts replicating the AES encryption from atoms/effects/persist.ts using the user digest as the key
Auth and chats stores use the encrypted persistence middleware
Recoil is NOT removed yet (both coexist temporarily)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The persistence must handle the bootstrap problem: on app load the digest is not available until login, so the store initializes empty and hydrates after authentication.

userStories.title: Rewire API layer and auth hooks to Zustand
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the API layer and authentication hooks to read/write from Zustand stores instead of Recoil atoms.
acceptanceCriteria: API/index.ts reads token from Zustand auth store instead of Recoil via recoil-nexus
API/refreshToken.ts updates Zustand auth store
usePrivateKey.ts reads from Zustand auth store
All Login page handlers (useHandleSubmit, useHandleRecovery, useHandleMnemonicSubmit, useHandleDigestUpdate, useHandleRegenerate) use Zustand stores
Register/index.tsx and Login/index.tsx use Zustand auth store
FocusHandler.tsx uses Zustand UI store for focus and session timeout
Login and register flows work end-to-end with Zustand
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewire Socket.IO and message hooks to Zustand
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Socket.IO hook and message-related hooks to use Zustand stores instead of Recoil atoms.
acceptanceCriteria: useSocket.ts reads/writes Zustand stores for user, chats, and UI state
useActiveChat.ts reads from Zustand chats store
useUpdateMessage.ts updates Zustand chats store
useDisplayTimestamp.ts uses Zustand UI store
useArchiveMessage, useMessageStatus, and useHandleDeleteChat handlers use Zustand stores
Real-time messaging works: send/receive messages, reactions, replies, typing indicators, read receipts all function correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove Recoil completely
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Recoil fully removed now that everything uses Zustand.
acceptanceCriteria: recoil, recoil-persist, and recoil-nexus are removed from dependencies
All files in src/atoms/ are deleted
RecoilRoot is removed from index.tsx
No remaining imports of recoil, recoil-nexus, or recoil-persist anywhere in the codebase
App is fully functional: can register, login, send/receive messages, all features work
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redesign login page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean, modern login page so that the app feels polished and trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page uses shadcn Card, Input, Button, Label components
Styled with Tailwind, no Bootstrap or SCSS classes
Fields: nick, password (with show/hide toggle via lucide Eye/EyeOff icons)
Login button submits the form
Recover account link/button triggers the recovery flow (mnemonic entry dialog using shadcn Dialog)
Create account link navigates to /register
Error states shown via shadcn toast or inline message
Responsive: looks good on both mobile and desktop viewports
Existing login logic (digest creation, API call, token decryption) works unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Redesign register page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean registration page that clearly presents the mnemonic seed phrase so I understand its importance.
acceptanceCriteria: Register page uses shadcn Card, Input, Button, Label components
Fields: nick (with availability check on blur), password (with show/hide toggle)
On submit, shadcn Dialog displays the 24-word mnemonic in a clear grid layout (4 columns x 6 rows)
Dialog warns user to write down the mnemonic and not screenshot it
I have saved my seed phrase button confirms and completes registration
Styled with Tailwind, no Bootstrap or SCSS
Existing registration logic (keypair generation, digest, API call) works unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build layout shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a responsive app layout with a sidebar for conversations and a main panel for chat.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-column layout: left sidebar (conversations list) + right main panel (active chat or empty state)
On desktop (>=768px): both panels visible side by side
On mobile (<768px): only one panel visible at a time. Conversations list is default, tapping a chat navigates to full-screen chat view, back button returns to conversations
Sidebar header shows app branding (diskreta.) and action buttons (new chat, settings)
Empty state in main panel when no chat is selected (e.g. Select a conversation)
Uses shadcn ScrollArea for scrollable panels
Styled with Tailwind, no Bootstrap grid or SCSS
Route structure: / shows conversations, /:activeChatId shows chat (mobile: full-screen, desktop: in main panel)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build conversations list
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my conversations sorted by most recent so I can quickly find and open chats.
acceptanceCriteria: Lists all chats from Zustand chats store, sorted by latest message timestamp (newest first)
Each conversation row shows: recipient nick(s), last message preview (or typing... if active), timestamp of last message
Unread message count shown as a badge on each conversation
Clicking a conversation navigates to /:chatId
Active conversation is visually highlighted
Delete conversation option (hover on desktop, swipe or long-press on mobile) with confirmation dialog
Uses shadcn Avatar, Badge, ScrollArea components
Styled with Tailwind
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build user search dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search for other users by nickname so I can start new conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: New chat button in sidebar header opens a shadcn Dialog
Dialog contains a search input with debounced search (existing API: GET /users?nick={query})
Results shown as a list of user nicks
Clicking a user creates or navigates to the existing chat with them
Empty state when no results found
Styled with Tailwind + shadcn components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build chat header
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see who I'm chatting with and have access to chat actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows recipient nick(s) for the active chat
On mobile: back button (lucide ArrowLeft icon) to return to conversations list
Dropdown menu (shadcn DropdownMenu) with Delete chat option
Delete chat shows confirmation dialog (shadcn AlertDialog) before executing
Styled with Tailwind + shadcn
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build ChatBody and message bubbles
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see messages in a chat with sent messages on the right and received on the left.
acceptanceCriteria: New ChatBody component replaces the old one, using Tailwind instead of Bootstrap classes
Messages rendered in chronological order (oldest at top, newest at bottom)
Sent messages aligned right with a distinct background color (e.g. bg-primary text-primary-foreground)
Received messages aligned left with a different background color (e.g. bg-muted)
Each message bubble shows text content and a small timestamp
Message bubbles have rounded corners and appropriate padding
Uses shadcn ScrollArea as the scrollable container
Styled with Tailwind, no Bootstrap classes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Focus ONLY on rendering message bubbles. Do not implement status icons, read receipts, auto-scroll, reactions, swipe, or long-press in this story. The old Message.tsx is 256 lines — do NOT try to port it all. Write a new simple MessageBubble component.

userStories.title: Add message status icons and read receipts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see delivery status on my sent messages and have received messages marked as read.
acceptanceCriteria: Sent messages show status icons next to the timestamp: Clock (outgoing), Check (sent), CheckCheck (delivered), blue CheckCheck (read) — using lucide-react icons
Status is read from the message object using the existing isMessageSent utility and status field
ChatBody emits read-msg socket event for visible received messages with status new (port existing logic from old ChatBody.tsx)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Reuse the existing read receipt logic from the old ChatBody.tsx useEffect. The status icon mapping: outgoing->Clock, sent->Check, delivered->CheckCheck, read->CheckCheck with text-blue-500 color.

userStories.title: Add auto-scroll, scroll-to-bottom, date separators, URL linking, and emoji rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want the chat to auto-scroll, show date separators, and render URLs and emoji nicely.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages (only if user is already at or near the bottom)
Scroll-to-bottom floating button (lucide ArrowDown icon) appears when scrolled up, click scrolls to bottom
Date separators between message groups showing Today, Yesterday, or full date
URLs in message text are detected and rendered as clickable links (opens in new tab)
Emoji-only messages (text containing only emoji, no other characters) rendered at larger font size
Styled with Tailwind
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: The URL regex and emoji regex can be copied from the old Message.tsx. The date separator logic can be copied from the old ChatBody.tsx (compare day of adjacent messages). ScrollToBottom is a simple floating button with an onClick that scrolls the container.

userStories.title: Build message input — text and send
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type and send encrypted text messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Textarea input at bottom of chat, auto-grows with content (max ~5 lines, then scrolls)
Send button (lucide Send icon) disabled when input is empty
Enter sends message, Shift+Enter inserts newline
Emits typing socket event while user is typing (existing debounce logic)
Existing encryption logic preserved: text encrypted with recipient RSA public key
Message sent via existing Socket.IO out-msg event with ACK handling
Styled with Tailwind + shadcn Button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build message input — media attachments
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send images in chat with automatic compression and encryption.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera/attachment button (lucide Paperclip or Camera icon) triggers file input for image capture/selection
Selected images are compressed using existing browser-image-compression logic (max 1MB)
HEIC images are converted to PNG using existing heic2any logic
Media is encrypted with a random AES key, the key itself RSA-encrypted with recipient public key (existing encryption logic)
Media preview shown in message input before sending
Sent media displays as a thumbnail in the message bubble
Styled with Tailwind
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build reply-to functionality
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reply to specific messages so conversations have clear context.
acceptanceCriteria: Swipe right on a message (50px threshold) triggers reply mode (existing useSwipe logic)
Reply bar appears above the message input showing the quoted message content and sender
Close button (lucide X icon) on reply bar cancels the reply
Sent reply includes replyingTo field in the message payload (existing logic)
Received replies show a quoted block above the message content with left border accent
Styled with Tailwind
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build emoji reactions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to react to messages with emoji.
acceptanceCriteria: Long-press (500ms) on a message shows reaction picker (existing useLongPress logic)
Reaction options displayed in a row: thumbs-up, heart, laughing, surprised, crying, praying-hands
Picker shown as shadcn Popover positioned near the message
Selecting a reaction sends it via existing out-reaction Socket.IO event
Received reactions displayed as small badges below the message
Tapping a reaction badge shows a shadcn Dialog listing who reacted with what
Styled with Tailwind + shadcn
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Build typing indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see when the other person is typing.
acceptanceCriteria: User is typing... text with animated dots shown at the bottom of the message list
Triggered by incoming typing Socket.IO event (existing logic)
Disappears after 500ms of no typing events (existing timeout logic)
Also shown as preview text in conversations list (typing... instead of last message)
Styled with Tailwind (CSS animation for dots)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: SMALL STORY. Only touch 2 files: (1) Rewrite src/pages/Main/Chat/Typing.tsx — replace Bootstrap classes with Tailwind, keep the existing logic. (2) In src/pages/Main/Conversations/index.tsx — the typing preview is already wired via chat.typing state, just ensure it shows 'typing...' text. Do NOT read MessageBubble.tsx or MessageInput.tsx — they are irrelevant to this story.

userStories.title: Build media viewer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view shared images in a full-screen viewer with zoom and pan.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping an image in a message opens a full-screen overlay
Overlay supports pinch-to-zoom and pan using react-zoom-pan-pinch library (already a dependency)
Close button (lucide X icon) or tap outside dismisses the viewer
Smooth fade-in/fade-out animation
Uses shadcn Dialog as the overlay container
Styled with Tailwind
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Rewrite src/pages/Main/Chat/Spotlight.tsx — replace @mui/icons-material Close with lucide X, replace SCSS animation classes with Tailwind/CSS transitions, wrap in shadcn Dialog. The Spotlight component is already rendered in Chat/index.tsx. Do NOT read MessageBubble.tsx fully — it already has onClick handler wired to setSpotlight.

userStories.title: Build settings dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to access settings to control theme, session timeout, and manage my data.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings gear icon (lucide Settings) in sidebar header opens a shadcn Dialog

Theme section: shadcn Select to choose light / dark / system. Selecting applies immediately by toggling dark class on <html> and respecting prefers-color-scheme for system. Persisted in user settings via Zustand.
Session timeout section: shadcn Select with options 15 seconds, 60 seconds, 10 minutes, 1 hour, Never. Existing FocusHandler logic applies.

Manage data section: Export button (copies encrypted JSON to clipboard using navigator.clipboard.writeText), Import button (paste JSON to restore), Delete account button (shadcn AlertDialog confirmation, calls DELETE /users/me). Do NOT use the copy-to-clipboard package — use the native navigator.clipboard API.
Styled with Tailwind + shadcn
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Remove legacy styling dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all legacy CSS/styling dependencies removed so the bundle is clean.
acceptanceCriteria: All files in src/styles/ deleted except globals.css
sass, bootstrap, react-bootstrap, react-bootstrap-icons, @emotion/react, @emotion/styled, @mui/icons-material removed from dependencies
No remaining imports of Bootstrap classes, SCSS files, Emotion styled components, or MUI icons anywhere in the codebase
lucide-react used for all icons
App renders correctly with only Tailwind + shadcn styling
No console warnings about missing stylesheets
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Remove legacy files and unused dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all old code removed so the codebase is clean and there is no dead code.
acceptanceCriteria: Old src/pages/ directory deleted (replaced by new pages)
Old src/components/ directory deleted (replaced by new components and shadcn ui)
Old src/hooks/ files that were fully replaced are deleted (keep any still imported)
Old src/constants/themes.ts deleted (replaced by light/dark/system)
react-json-tree, react-device-detect, use-deep-compare, copy-to-clipboard (replaced by navigator.clipboard), serve, patch-package, react-error-overlay removed
No unused imports or dead code files remain
pnpm dev and pnpm build both succeed without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add push notification support (server)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the server to send Web Push notifications when a recipient is offline so they know they have a new message.
acceptanceCriteria: web-push package installed in apps/server
VAPID key pair generated and stored as environment variables (VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_EMAIL)
New route POST /api/push/subscribe accepts a PushSubscription object and stores it on the user document in MongoDB (new pushSubscription field on User model)
New route DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe removes the stored push subscription from the user document
New route GET /api/push/vapid-public-key returns the VAPID public key for client-side subscription

In the existing out-msg handler in server.ts: when recipient is offline (not in shared.onlineUsers), send a Web Push notification with payload { title: Diskreta, body: You have a new message } with NO message content and NO sender name
Push send failures (expired subscription etc) are caught and the subscription is removed from the user document
Existing message queuing to MongoDB is unchanged (push is in addition to the queue)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add push notification support (client)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive a push notification when I get a message while the app is in the background, so I don't miss conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Service Worker file at public/sw.js handles push events and shows a notification using self.registration.showNotification()
Notification click opens or focuses the app window
On first login after registration, the app prompts the user to enable notifications (browser permission prompt). If granted, subscribes and sends the PushSubscription to POST /api/push/subscribe
On logout or account deletion, calls DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe
Toggle in settings dialog to enable/disable push notifications. Default: on (prompt shown). If disabled, unsubscribes.
Push subscription is refreshed on each login (subscriptions can expire)
Service Worker registered in app entry point
Works in Chrome and Firefox
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
